title: oldenlandia adscensionis
intro: Oldenlandia adscensionis, also known as "five-finger grass" or "Asian tick trefoil," is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oldenlandia adscensionis include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections, such as bronchitis and tuberculosis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
